note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Creating a Clean Air Environment: Final Clearance and Handover — Quiz

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of final clearance in air quality remediation?
options: To begin the remediation process and install containment structures.
To raise the building's humidity before occupants return.
To confirm that all contamination has been removed and the space is safe for occupancy.
To market the property to prospective tenants.
questions.sourceRef: M1 — The Importance of Final Clearance in Air Quality Remediation

questions.questionText: According to the lesson, indoor PM2.5 levels are generally considered healthy when they are below which value?
options: Below 12 µg/m³
Below 850 µg/m³
Below 500 µg/m³
Below 60 µg/m³
questions.sourceRef: M2 — The Role of Air Quality Testing in Final Clearance

questions.questionText: When air is sampled for mould spores, a successful final clearance result (Condition 1) typically demonstrates that:
options: No spores of any type are present anywhere in the building.
Indoor spore levels and types are similar to or lower than outdoor levels or non-impacted indoor reference areas.
Indoor spore levels are around twice the outdoor levels.
Spore levels are highest in the previously contaminated source area.
questions.sourceRef: M2 — The Role of Air Quality Testing in Final Clearance

questions.questionText: In air quality testing, carbon dioxide (CO2) levels are mainly used as an indicator of:
options: The concentration of mould spores in the air.
The structural strength of the building.
The level of VOCs released from paints.
Ventilation adequacy relative to occupancy.
questions.sourceRef: M2 — The Role of Air Quality Testing in Final Clearance

questions.questionText: Under what conditions should final clearance air quality testing be carried out?
options: After remediation and cleaning are complete and containment structures have been removed, reflecting normal occupancy.
While remediation is still underway and containment is still in place.
Only during periods of high outdoor humidity.
Before any cleaning of the affected area has begun.
questions.sourceRef: M2 — The Role of Air Quality Testing in Final Clearance

questions.questionText: Why should the HVAC system be inspected during final clearance?
options: Because HVAC filters have no effect on indoor air quality.
To measure the outdoor air temperature.
It can be a hidden source of contamination if ducts, vents, or filters were not properly cleaned.
Because it is the only place where mould can grow.
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Visual Inspections and Documentation for Final Handover

questions.questionText: According to the lesson, relative humidity should be kept below what level to help prevent mould proliferation?
options: Below 30%
Below 60%
Below 85%
Below 100%
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Final Clearance Equipment and Tools

questions.questionText: What should a comprehensive handover report include?
options: Only a verbal summary, with no written record of the work.
The contractor's pricing for future unrelated projects.
A guarantee that mould can never return under any circumstances.
The final clearance inspection and testing results, confirmation that the clearance criteria were met, and photographic documentation.
questions.sourceRef: M5 — Best Practices for Handover and Client Communication
